name: Pose Repeatability Study
description: A robotics engineer must conduct an ISO 9283-inspired pose repeatability test on a simulated robot arm in CoppeliaSim to validate its kinematic model and control logic. Using the ZMQ Remote API on port 23000, programmatically drive the robot through at least 30 homing-and-return cycles for each of at least 3 distinct target joint configurations (spatially separated by at least 0.05m in Cartesian space). Record the settled Cartesian end-effector positions for each trial and compute repeatability metrics including mean position, deviations, and RP values. Export the trial data (minimum 90 trials) to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/repeatability_data.csv with columns: pose_id, trial_id, target_x, target_y, target_z, measured_x, measured_y, measured_z, and deviation_mm. Generate a summary report at /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/repeatability_report.json including fields for total_poses, trials_per_pose, worst_rp_mm, best_rp_mm, mean_rp_mm, overall_rp_mm, and iso_compliant (boolean, true if total_poses >= 3 and trials_per_pose >= 30).
